children.heading: Conditions for approval of proposed project
children.label: a Conditions for approval of proposed project

children.text: The project complies with all applicable Federal laws (including regulations).

The project is consistent with the applicable resource management plan and with any watershed or subsequent plan developed pursuant to the resource management plan and approved by the Secretary concerned.
The project has been approved by the resource advisory committee in accordance with , including the procedures issued under subsection (e) of that section.section 7125 of this title
A project description has been submitted by the resource advisory committee to the Secretary concerned in accordance with .section 7123 of this title
The project will improve the maintenance of existing infrastructure, implement stewardship objectives that enhance forest ecosystems, and restore and improve land health and water quality.

children.heading: Request for payment by county
children.label: 1 Request for payment by county
children.text: The Secretary concerned may request the resource advisory committee submitting a proposed project to agree to the use of project funds to pay for any environmental review, consultation, or compliance with applicable environmental laws required in connection with the project.

children.heading: Conduct of environmental review
children.label: 2 Conduct of environmental review
children.text: If a payment is requested under paragraph (1) and the resource advisory committee agrees to the expenditure of funds for this purpose, the Secretary concerned shall conduct environmental review, consultation, or other compliance responsibilities in accordance with Federal laws (including regulations).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If a resource advisory committee does not agree to the expenditure of funds under paragraph (1), the project shall be deemed withdrawn from further consideration by the Secretary concerned pursuant to this subchapter.

children.heading: Effect of withdrawal
children.label: B Effect of withdrawal
children.text: A withdrawal under subparagraph (A) shall be deemed to be a rejection of the project for purposes of .section 7127(c) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A decision by the Secretary concerned to reject a proposed project shall be at the sole discretion of the Secretary concerned.

children.heading: No administrative appeal or judicial review
children.label: B No administrative appeal or judicial review
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, a decision by the Secretary concerned to reject a proposed project shall not be subject to administrative appeal or judicial review.

children.heading: Notice of rejection
children.label: C Notice of rejection
children.text: Not later than 30 days after the date on which the Secretary concerned makes the rejection decision, the Secretary concerned shall notify in writing the resource advisory committee that submitted the proposed project of the rejection and the reasons for rejection.

children.heading: Notice of project approval
children.label: 2 Notice of project approval
children.text: The Secretary concerned shall publish in the Federal Register notice of each project approved under subsection (a) if the notice would be required had the project originated with the Secretary.

children.heading: Source and conduct of project
children.label: d Source and conduct of project
children.text: Once the Secretary concerned accepts a project for review under , the acceptance shall be deemed a Federal action for all purposes.section 7123 of this title

children.heading: Cooperation
children.label: 1 Cooperation
children.text: Notwithstanding chapter 63 of title 31, using project funds the Secretary concerned may enter into contracts, grants, and cooperative agreements with States and local governments, private and nonprofit entities, and landowners and other persons to assist the Secretary in carrying out an approved project.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For any project involving a contract authorized by paragraph (1) the Secretary concerned may elect a source for performance of the contract on a best value basis.

children.label: i
children.text: the technical demands and complexity of the work to be done;

children.label: I
children.text: the ecological objectives of the project; and

children.label: II
children.text: the sensitivity of the resources being treated;

children.label: iii
children.text: the past experience by the contractor with the type of work being done, using the type of equipment proposed for the project, and meeting or exceeding desired ecological conditions; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the commitment of the contractor to hiring highly qualified workers and local residents.
to road maintenance, decommissioning, or obliteration; or
to restoration of streams and watersheds.
